title: Rebalance deltas include unrelated earlier same-block reserve changes
reasoning: The handler computes a rebalance's token deltas by subtracting reserves from the previous block from the post-rebalance pool reserves. If the same pool had any swap, mint/burn, or other reserve-changing transaction earlier in the same block, those changes are also included in `amount0Delta`/`amount1Delta`, inflating `notionalUsd` and `rewardUsd` for the RebalanceEvent. The event is block/log scoped, but the baseline is only block scoped.
recommendation: Capture the pre-rebalance reserves from the in-batch Pool state before the first UpdateReserves belonging to the rebalance transaction, for example with a tx-scoped scratch entity keyed by chainId/poolId/txHash, and have the Rebalanced handler consume that instead of `blockNumber - 1`. Keep the RPC path only as an explicit unknown fallback.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The reviewed feature declares no linked tests, and the handler comments/tests exercise the simple case where the prior-block reserves equal the actual pre-rebalance reserves. They do not cover an earlier same-block reserve update before the rebalance transaction.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an indexer handler test that processes an UpdateReserves for the same pool earlier in block N, then the two UpdateReserves plus Rebalanced for a rebalance later in block N, and asserts RebalanceEvent amount deltas only reflect the rebalance transaction, not the earlier same-block update.
minimumFixScope: indexer-envio/src/handlers/fpmm/state-sync.ts plus any schema/test support needed for a tx-scoped pre-rebalance reserve scratch record.

history.note: Rebalanced now uses same-tx pre-UpdateReserves scratch for deltas with RPC previous-block only as fallback; regression added.
